prompt: SnapshotStore publishes a checksummed file by writing a staging file, durably flushing it, and asking a FileSystemAdapter to atomically replace the live snapshot. A failed publication must never make the previously committed snapshot unreadable or change its contents. Fix the implementation so an open, write, flush, or atomic-replace failure preserves the last good snapshot, retains the original PlatformError operation and native error code, and cleans up any abandoned staging file. Recovery must remain safe to call repeatedly. Do not change the snapshot format, weaken checksum validation, bypass the FileSystemAdapter, or modify the tests or Makefile. Run `make test` from the repository root.
